_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in applying security models (e.g., Bell-LaPadula model, Biba integrity model, Clark-Wilson integrity model).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including policy development, role and responsibility definition, escalation protocols, exception management, and board-level model risk reporting.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AG-K-001 (AG, cosine: 0.478), RM-S-002 (RM, cosine: 0.470).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.4/10 (raw 0.544). Shared vocabulary: [model, skill]. DCWF-only terms [applying, bell, biba, clark, integrity, lapadula] vs WIDAI-only terms [board, definition, designing, development, escalation, exception] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.848 (P=0.832, R=0.865). R exceeds P by 0.033 — the WIDAI KSA's 26 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 14 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.7%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in applying security models (e.g., Bell-LaPadula model, Biba integrity mod" and "Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.483 < STS 0.544 (gap -0.061). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [applying, bell, biba] and [board, definition, designing] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.8%. Shared tokens: [model, skill]. DCWF-only: [applying, bell, biba, clark, integrity, lapadula]. WIDAI-only: [board, definition, designing, development, escalation, exception].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.7% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
